name: Retro To-Do
description: A retro-themed to-do app styled like an old CRT monitor with bulge, scanlines, and phosphor glow. Kanban columns (To-do, In Progress, Completed), calendar by due date, drag-and-drop, and modal task creation. All data in browser localStorage — no authentication.

features.title: Implement task domain model and localStorage persistence layer
features.description:
In the Retro To-Do project workspace under `projects/retro-to-do`, add a single source of truth for tasks stored only in `localStorage` (no server APIs, no auth).

**Task shape (TypeScript-friendly):**
- `id`: string UUID v4 generated on the client when a task is created.
- `title`: string, required, trim whitespace, max 200 characters.
- `description`: string, optional, max 5000 characters.
- `priority`: one of `low`, `medium`, `high` (stored as lowercase string literals).
- `dueDate`: optional ISO 8601 date string (`YYYY-MM-DD`) representing the user's local calendar date; allow null/undefined for "no due date".
- `status`: exactly one of `todo`, `in_progress`, `completed` — maps Kanban columns To-do → `todo`, In Progress → `in_progress`, Completed → `completed`.
- `createdAt` / `updatedAt`: ISO 8601 timestamps (strings) maintained on create/update.

**Storage key:** Use a stable namespaced key e.g. `retro-todo:tasks:v1`. If migrating later, bump version suffix.

**API surface (exported functions/hooks however the stack prefers):**
- `loadTasks()`, `saveTasks(Task[])`, optimistic updates after every mutation.
- `createTask(payload)`, `updateTask(id, partial)`, `deleteTask(id)`, `moveTask(id, newStatus)` (preserve task order within a column deterministically — e.g. append to bottom of destination column).

**Resilience:** If `localStorage` is missing or JSON parse fails, fall back to empty array and optionally show a subtle non-blocking warning. Never throw uncaught exceptions.

**Out of scope:** Sync, multi-device, encryption, IndexedDB — localStorage only for MVP.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- A dedicated module encapsulates Task type and all read/write helpers
- Tasks persist across full page reloads (same browser profile)
- New tasks default `status` to `todo`
- `priority` validates to only `low` | `medium` | `high`
- `dueDate`, when provided, validates as calendar date format `YYYY-MM-DD`
- Corrupt stored JSON recovers gracefully to empty tasks without crashing
- `updatedAt` changes on edits and moves; `createdAt` is immutable after create
- Deleting removes task from storage and from any rendered view once implemented

features.title: Create Retro To-Do page shell with header, Add Task entry, and view region
features.description:
Add the main Retro To-Do route within `projects/retro-to-do` (follow that project's routing conventions — Vite SPA, Next.js nested route, etc.). The page occupies the viewport and establishes structure before Kanban/calendar specifics.

**Header area:** Left: app title "Retro To-Do" or similar retro label. Primary action: prominent "Add Task" button (keyboard-focusable).

**View region:** A single main zone where either the Kanban board or the calendar view will render (implemented in downstream features).

**Accessibility:** Landmark roles (`header`, `main`), visible focus outlines, logical tab order to Add Task.

**Out of scope in this ticket:** CRT visual polish; Kanban columns; modal fields; calendar grid — separate features.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Navigating to the Retro To-Do entry route renders header + main view region without errors
- "Add Task" is visible and activates (opens modal once modal feature exists)
- Layout does not horizontally overflow at 375px viewport width
- Semantic HTML landmarks are present (`header`, `main`)
- Focus-visible styles exist for interactive controls

features.title: Apply CRT monitor visuals: bezel, bulge, scanlines, and phosphor glow
features.description:
Purely visual/styling enhancement on top of the existing Retro To-Do shell.

**Techniques:** Bulge/barrel on inner screen (subtle ~3–10% distortion max). Scanlines via low-opacity horizontal gradient overlay. Phosphor glow / vignette; monospace fonts. Optional very subtle animated grain — respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.

**Structure:** Outer monitor bezel, inner glass clipped region containing app content.

**Out of scope:** Business logic or localStorage changes.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Page reads as CRT-inspired (bezels + scanlines + glow)
- Inner content shows curved/bulge screen effect or equivalent CRT evocation
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` disables or dampens animated noise
- Body text readable at ~16px on desktop and mobile
- Interactive controls remain tappable (not clipped by bezel/mask)

features.title: Build Kanban board with To-do, In Progress, and Completed columns
features.description:
Render three columns wired to persisted tasks from the localStorage layer.

**Column headers:** exact labels "To-do", "In progress", "Completed".

**Cards:** Title (required), priority badge (distinct per level), description truncated or expandable, due date formatted or hidden if absent. Deterministic order (e.g. newest at bottom).

**Empty columns:** Retro-styled "No tasks" placeholder.

**Responsive:** Stack columns on narrow viewports or horizontal scroll — implementer chooses.

**Out of scope:** Drag-and-drop, modal, calendar — separate tickets.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Three labeled columns with clear separation
- Tasks map `todo` / `in_progress` / `completed` to correct columns
- Priority visible per card with distinct styling
- Missing description or due date does not break layout
- Empty column shows empty-state UI
- Board updates after store mutations

features.title: Add task creation modal with title, description, priority, and due date
features.description:
Wire header "Add Task" to a modal dialog.

**Fields:** Title (required, max 200). Description (optional textarea, max 5000). Priority (required, default medium: Low/Medium/High). Due date (optional `type="date"`).

**Actions:** Create saves via task store to `todo`; Cancel closes without persisting. Focus trap; Esc closes. CRT-friendly backdrop.

**Out of scope:** Editing existing tasks (separate ticket if needed).
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Modal opens from Add Task; Cancel does not create a task
- Empty title shows validation; no persist
- Valid submit adds task in To-do with correct fields
- Omitted due date stores no due date
- ESC closes; focus trap works; scrollable body on short viewports

features.title: Implement drag-and-drop for moving tasks between Kanban columns
features.description: Use dnd-kit, native DnD, or equivalent. Dropping on a column updates `status` to `todo` / `in_progress` / `completed`. Call `moveTask` / equivalent; update `updatedAt`. Visual lift/highlight during drag. Cancel invalid drop without corrupting storage. Prefer keyboard-accessible DnD or a documented fallback (e.g. move menu).
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Drag between columns updates persisted status and survives reload
- Invalid drop restores visual state; storage stays consistent
- `updatedAt` reflects successful move
- Visible drag affordance (ghost, shadow, or highlight)

features.title: Implement month calendar view keyed by due dates
features.description: Alternate main view: month grid with prev/next and localized month label. Place tasks in cells where `dueDate` matches local calendar day. Tasks without due date do not appear on the grid. Multi-task days: compact list, +more, or drawer — minimal viable UX. Same task store as Kanban.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Correct weekday alignment and month boundaries
- Prev/next works across month/year boundaries
- Due-dated tasks appear in the right cells; no bogus defaults for undated tasks
- New task with due date appears in calendar after modal submit
- Month label updates with navigation

features.title: Add Board versus Calendar toggle and persist selected view preference
features.description: Segmented control or tabs: "Board" / "Calendar". Persist `board` | `calendar` under e.g. `retro-todo:ui:viewPreference`. Corrupt value defaults to board. Add Task remains available on calendar view.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Toggle swaps main region between Kanban and calendar without route change
- Active state visible; survives reload
- Preference key separate from tasks storage
- Add Task opens same modal from calendar view
